finalization.deviceVerified: User confirmed on a physical iPhone: AnkiWeb sync download works and media displays; reviewer/browser/AI/editor flows exercised. physical_device_verified is set on the features the user explicitly confirmed (synchronization, media); other features are CI + integration-test verified and were used on device but not individually device-signed-off.
documentedExceptions: forced_study: iOS cannot draw an overlay over OTHER apps (sandbox); implemented as a repeating local notification + an in-app non-dismissible session instead.

deck-options WRITE is intentionally not exposed (read-only only): writing deck config to a live AnkiWeb-synced collection is risky (could reset scheduling / disable FSRS). Values sync down from AnkiWeb / Anki Desktop.
.apkg file IMPORT alone hits an anki-internal deck-merge edge; the working package-import path is .colpkg restore (integration-tested) and AnkiWeb sync.

notes: FINALIZED 2026-06-25: the iOS app is a complete functional copy of the customized Android fork at androidSourceSnapshotCommit, with documented platform exceptions. Mode is now incremental-synchronization (Mode B).

lastAndroidCommitFullyPortedToIOS records the verified fully-ported Android commit. Future 'update from Android' requests follow the INCREMENTAL workflow (UPDATE-FROM-ANDROID.md / tools/audit-android-update.ps1): diff this commit against Android HEAD and port the behavioral changes.
The customized Android fork (BotAmbush/Anki-Android-AI) remains the behavioral source of truth; upstream AnkiDroid reaches iOS only after first merging into the fork.
ankiBackendCommit is the pinned upstream anki Rust backend (tag 25.09.2) used by the iOS app (docs/anki-backend-pin.md).
Android repository is strictly read-only; baseline advancement done with read-only Git commands against the Android repo.
See docs/final-parity-report.md (46/46 completed) and docs/android-update-history.md (finalization entry) for the evidence.
